title: Bigelovia tenera
intro: Bigelovia tenera, also known as Delphinium exaltatum or larkspur, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bigelovia tenera include delphiniine alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory problems and other conditions, although caution should be exercised when handling the plant due to its potential toxicity.
taxonomy: Bigelovia tenera is classified as a species within the genus Bigelovia, which is a group of flowering plants in the family Boraginaceae. The Boraginaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. The Boraginaceae family is part of the more general group known as the asterids, which is one of the two main groups of flowering plants. The asterids group is also known as the eudicots, which are flowering plants that have four or five petals and are often characterized by the presence of a distinct calyx. Overall, Bigelovia tenera's classification reflects its position within a diverse and widespread group of flowering plants.
